title: Remote messenger hook v0
description: Build .harness/bin/notify.py reading .harness/notify_config.json (provider: telegram|generic webhook; url/token). Ships DISABLED with dry_run=true; supports --dry-run explicitly; never commits secrets (config is gitignored). Optionally wire as a Claude Code Notification/Stop hook AFTER human approval. ZCode parity item #2.
acceptance_criteria: dry-run prints the payload without any network call
activation path documented and listed under state.json human_gates
no secret material tracked by git

notes.note: plan: build .harness/bin/notify.py (init/status/send subcommands, house style like lock.py/recontext.py) shipping DISABLED (enabled=false, dry_run=true, empty url); send() dry-runs by default and only reaches urllib.request when enabled&&!dry_run&&url non-empty (unreachable with shipped config); redact token everywhere; document Claude Code hook activation path clearly gated behind human approval; add one README bin/ line; validate (a)-(h) dry-run only, zero network.

HANDOFF -> verifier: REPLAY (a)-(h), all dry-run/no-network: (a) git check-ignore -v .harness/notify_config.json -> '.gitignore:16:.harness/notify_config.json .harness/notify_config.json' exit=0 (ignored, confirmed both pre- and post-existence). (b) python3 .harness/bin/notify.py init -> 'created: .../.harness/notify_config.json (enabled=false, dry_run=true, provider=generic, url/token empty)...'; re-running init -> 'refused: ... already exists. Not overwriting...' exit=1. (c) python3 .harness/bin/notify.py status -> enabled:False dry_run:True provider:generic url:(empty) token:(empty) effective mode:DRY RUN(config.enabled is not true) -- token NEVER printed raw. (d) python3 .harness/bin/notify.py send --message 'smoke test T-005' -> 'DRY RUN (reason: config.enabled is not true) -- no network call made.' + provider/url/full JSON payload printed, exit=0; grep notify_dry_run .harness/logs/events.jsonl -> event present with reason='config.enabled is not true'. (e) python3 .harness/bin/notify.py send --message x --dry-run -> same dry-run banner, reason='--dry-run flag passed', exit=0; second notify_dry_run event confirmed (grep -c =2 total). (f) NO NETWORK PROOF -- shipped .harness/notify_config.json literally contains enabled=false (bool, not string) AND dry_run=true (bool) AND url='' (empty string); asserted programmatically (python3 -c assert cfg[enabled] is False and cfg[dry_run] is True and cfg[url]=='' -> ASSERTIONS_PASSED). send()'s only network branch (urllib.request.urlopen) is gated behind _should_dry_run() returning False, which requires enabled==True AND dry_run==False AND url non-empty -- verified via pure-function unit calls (no send() invocation, no network): shipped config -> (True,'config.enabled is not true'); 4 hypothetical configs each toggling one guard independently confirm only the fully-live hypothetical (never persisted, never sent) returns dry_run=False. (g) python3 -m py_compile .harness/bin/notify.py -> exit 0, no output (ran twice, both clean). (h) git status --short -> ' M .harness/README.md', ' M .harness/blackboard.json', ' M .harness/tasks/T-005.json', '?? .harness/bin/notify.py' -- notify_config.json NOT listed; git ls-files | grep notify_config -> no output (not tracked); grep -in secret/password/api_key/sekret .harness/bin/notify.py -> no matches (only empty-string placeholders ship in code/config). ADDITIONAL: notify.py --help and send --help render correctly (argparse subcommands init/status/send wired); docstring documents full activation path incl. example Stop/Notification hook JSON block for .claude/settings.json, labeled 'DO NOT WIRE THIS HOOK JSON until the human gate approves activation' in two places; .claude/settings.json itself was NOT touched (verified: only Read, never Edit/Write on that path this session). README.md diff is exactly one added line in the bin/ tree matching existing style (see git diff .harness/README.md). Artifacts recorded via two separate --artifact calls (G8-safe): .harness/bin/notify.py, .harness/README.md -- both confirmed present in blackboard show T-005. EXPECTED vs ACTUAL: expected the shipped config's three guards (enabled/dry_run/url) to independently force dry-run and block urllib.request; actual matches exactly, confirmed both via CLI-level replay (d/e) and pure-function unit isolation of _should_dry_run (f) without ever calling send() against a live-toggled config. No secrets were ever written to any file -- init only writes the empty-string TEMPLATE constant.

VERIFIED (safety lens: no network happened, none reachable, no secret leaks). AC1 dry-run-no-network: 'send --message verifier probe' -> DRY RUN reason 'config.enabled is not true', prints payload, exit0; CROSS-RUN PROOF notify_dry_run=3, notify_sent=0, notify_failed=0 (zero network EVER attempted). AC2 activation-gated: docstring L54-99 documents full activation path with 2x 'DO NOT WIRE...until human gate' warnings; state.json human_gates lists 'activating or sending remote webhook notifications (T-005) for the first time'. AC3 no-secret-in-git: notify_config.json gitignored (.gitignore:16 via git check-ignore), git ls-files|grep notify EMPTY (not tracked), TEMPLATE ships empty-string url/token, token NEVER printed raw (status shows '(empty)'/'***set***'; _redact_url scrubs token substrings). CODE-LEVEL NET AUDIT (diversity): grep urllib|socket|http.client|requests -> ONLY urllib at L266-267 inside cmd_send LIVE branch, reached only when _should_dry_run()==False (requires enabled==True AND dry_run==False AND url non-empty); shipped enabled=false trips the FIRST guard -> return 0 before urllib; 3 independent guards = defense in depth; no other net primitive. init refuses overwrite (exit1). .claude/settings.json git diff EMPTY (hook wiring NOT done, human-gated). py_compile OK; README bin/ line 40 present. OBSERVATION (non-defect): git ls-files shows notify.py currently UNTRACKED (??) not 'tracked' - deferred-commit working-tree state, same as session.py/recontext.py; safety property (config absent from git) holds regardless.
